code:
import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut done_skip = false
    while not done_skip
      if i >= n
        done_skip = true
      else
        match string.byte_at(s, i)
        when Some(c)
          if c == 32 or c == 9 or c == 13 or c == 10
            i = i + 1
          else
            done_skip = true
          end
        when None
          done_skip = true
        end
      end
    end
    if i < n
      mut sign = 1
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(c)
        if c == 45
          sign = -1
          i = i + 1
        else if c == 43
          i = i + 1
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      mut num = 0
      mut done_num = false
      while not done_num
        if i >= n
          done_num = true
        else
          match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(c)
            if c >= 48 and c <= 57
              num = num * 10 + (c - 48)
              i = i + 1
            else
              done_num = true
            end
          when None
            done_num = true
          end
        end
      end
      result = list.push(result, num * sign)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn print_pairs(_ xs: List<Int>, _ ys: List<Int>) -> Unit
  let n = list.len(xs)
  mut k = 0
  while k < n
    match list.get(xs, k)
    when Some(x)
      match list.get(ys, k)
      when Some(y)
        let s = x + y
        println("#{x}+#{y}=#{s}")
      when None
        ()
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    k = k + 1
  end
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(l1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(l2)
    print_pairs(parse_ints(l1), parse_ints(l2))
  when None
    ()
  end
when None
  ()
end
